channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: What injured ankle? Ja Morant flies around court helping Grizzlies take West's 8th seed
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Two-time All-Star Ja Morant promised a sprained right ankle wouldn't keep him out of Memphis' play-in game against Dallas with the Western Conference's last postseason berth up for grabs Friday night. Morant did more than just play.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Grizzlies beat the Mavericks 120-106 to set up a 1st-round playoff series with the Thunder
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Ja Morant shrugged off a sprained right ankle to score 22 points and add nine assists and the Memphis Grizzlies beat the Dallas Mavericks 120-106 on Friday night to advance to the NBA playoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Boston Marathon and city insist all are welcome. But some runners say politics will keep them home
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The Boston Marathon and Mayor Michelle Wu insisted on Friday that international runners and other foreign visitors remain welcome in the city and said there is no evidence that travel for this year’s race has fallen off in the face of increased border scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Herro scores 30 points to lead Heat past Hawks 123-114 in OT in play-in and into NBA playoffs
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — After making NBA Play-In Tournament history by winning two road games, the Miami Heat won't be afraid of facing No. 1 seed Cleveland in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Edman hits leadoff HR filling in for Ohtani and Yamamoto strikes out 10 as Dodgers beat Texas 3-0
item.description: Yoshinobu Yamamoto matched his career high with 10 strikeouts over seven innings after Tommy Edman led off the game with a home run and the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the Texas Rangers 3-0.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Hawkins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yankees' Jazz Chisholm Jr. suspended 1 game following ejection, violation of social media policy
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — New York Yankees second baseman Jazz Chisholm Jr. was suspended for one game and fined by Major League Baseball on Friday following his ejection during a game at the Tampa Bay Rays and violation of MLB’s social media policy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindblad joins Buhai atop the leaderboard in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ingrid Lindblad took a share of the lead into the weekend at El Caballero Country Club in only the fourth start of her rookie season on the LPGA Tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tucker hits 2-run HR, Cubs score 6 in 8th after giving up 10 in top half to beat Diamondbacks 13-11
item.description: Kyle Tucker had the fans on their feet, roaring and pumping their fists as he rounded the bases after hitting the go-ahead two-run homer in the eighth inning.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Seligman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Shohei Ohtani away from the Los Angeles Dodgers for birth of his first child
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is away from the Los Angeles Dodgers for the birth of the two-way superstar’s first child. Manager Dave Roberts said before the Dodgers’ series opener Friday night in Texas that Ohtani was with his wife and going on MLB’s paternity list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Justin Thomas makes two late birdies to restore his lead at Hilton Head
item.description: HILTON HEAD ISLAND, S.C. (AP) — Justin Thomas made a pair of birdies on the crusty, breezy back nine at Harbour Town to salvage a 2-under 69 for a two-shot lead Friday at the RBC Heritage, his first 36-hole lead in more than four years.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saints All-Pro tackle Ryan Ramczyk, 30, retires after battling through knee injury
item.description: New Orleans Saints tackle Ryan Ramczyk is stepping away from football after eight years, the All-Pro announced Thursday in a social media post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saints injured former All-Pro right tackle Ryan Ramczyk retires after missing entire 2024 season
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Saints right tackle Ryan Ramczyk, a 2017 first-round draft choice and former All-Pro who sat out all of last season because of a lingering knee injury, has announced his retirement from pro football.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine Democrats trying to amend state constitution to codify allowing trans athletes in girls' sports
item.description: Maine Democrats took a step toward constitutionalizing trans athletes' protections the day after the DOJ announced a lawsuit against the state over the issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kawhi Leonard is fresh in mind and body as the streaking Los Angeles Clippers open the playoffs
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Kawhi Leonard is fresh in mind and body this year, no longer relegated to watching from the sideline when the Los Angeles Clippers begin the postseason.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox DFA Mike Clevinger, call-up Steven Wilson from AAA
item.description: The Chicago White Sox have designated Mike Clevinger for assignment and called up reliever Steven Wilson from Triple-A, while Wilson remains on the 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Golden State Warriors warn fans of counterfeit playoff tickets
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors narrowly clinched a spot in the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a home win Tuesday night against the Memphis Grizzlies. Scammers, the team warned in a new advisory, are also looking to win big by selling fans counterfeit tickets.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Cubs bullpen works 5 shutout innings, Hoerner's go-ahead triple in 10th hands Padres first home loss, 2-1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The San Diego Padres hadn't loss a home game yet on the young season. Until Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Tyler Soderstrom hits a pair of 3-run home runs and the Athletics beat the White Sox 12-3
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Tyler Soderstrom hit a pair of three-run home runs, his major league-leading seventh and eighth homers of the season, and the Athletics beat the Chicago White Sox 12-3 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Young Bulls, Lonzo Ball and future of his highly-touted twin sons with Carlos Boozer
item.description: Carlos Boozer recently spoke with Jarrett Payton to discuss the Chicago Bulls' young team, Lonzo Ball's injury, and his sons' basketball success, with both Cameron and Cayden Boozer being highly-touted high school recruits and planning to attend Duke University.
item.dc:creator: Jarrett Payton, WGN 9

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors' playoff seeding set after loss against Clippers in regular-season finale
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors' playoff path is set. They will play in the NBA Play-In Tournament following a home overtime loss to the Los Angeles Clippers in Sunday's regular-season finale. With the loss, the Warriors will face the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Report: Cubs' Justin Steele to undergo elbow surgery, done for the season
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Cubs were dealt a heavy blow on the injury front Sunday evening. All-Star starting pitcher Justin Steele is done for the season, according to the Chicago Sun-Times.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs have big night in 16-0 rout of Dodgers, handing LA the worst home shutout loss in franchise history
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers franchise has been around since 1884, going back to its days in Brooklyn. And no team had ever done to the Dodgers in front of their home crowd what the Cubs did at Dodger Stadium on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: MIKE DIGIOVANNA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, NewsNation
